changes.title: Room creation is now idempotent / crash-safe
changes.description: ensureRoomV3 wrote 5 files sequentially with no existence check. A mid-flight failure left rooms half-initialised and two concurrent joins could collide. Each file is now checked before writing, so a partial creation is safely resumable on any retry.

changes.title: Concurrent user registration no longer overwrites peers
changes.description: registerUser read users/index.json once, mutated the in-memory object and wrote it back with the original SHA. When two registrations raced, the winner's write silently erased the loser's entry. The index update now retries up to 5 times with a fresh re-read-merge-verify cycle, matching GitHub's optimistic-concurrency model.

changes.title: Edited and deleted messages no longer hidden by CDN cache
changes.description: loadCellsRange only used the live GitHub API for the very last cell; the second-to-last cell was fetched via raw.githubusercontent.com which caches for up to 5 minutes. Edits or deletes landed in that cell were invisible until the cache expired. The API path now covers the last two cells on every incremental sync.

changes.title: Hyper-burst window starts after sync finishes, not before
changes.description: After resetting the master flag the hyper-burst end-time was calculated from the pre-sync timestamp, effectively shortening the window by the sync's own duration (often 1–2 s). It is now calculated from Date.now() after the sync and reset complete.

changes.title: Sound toggle state no longer drifts from localStorage
changes.description: The notification-center sound button tracked state in a closure variable initialised once at startup. If the admin settings modal or another tab wrote a different value to localStorage the button label and behaviour diverged silently. The button now reads localStorage on every click and on every label refresh.

changes.title: Presence write pressure reduced during hyper-burst
changes.description: markRead was throttled at 1 500 ms while the hyper-burst poll fires every 500 ms, stacking presence writes with log-flush writes under the GitHub rate limit. The throttle has been raised to 3 000 ms.

changes.title: DM room name collision now surfaced as an error
changes.description: openDMWithUser derived a deterministic room name from two user IDs and joined it without checking its type. In the unlikely event a public room shared that name, private DM messages would flow into the public room. A type-mismatch check now aborts the operation with a clear error message.

changes.title: checkNewMessages safe before guest identity is ready
changes.description: checkNewMessages used a hard property access on localGuest before initGuestIdentity had run, which could throw in fast-startup paths. Replaced with optional chaining so the function exits cleanly if called too early.

changes.title: Message retry now uses the serialised write queue
changes.description: retryMessage had its own direct ghGetJsonWithSha + ghPutJson path that completely bypassed _msgWriteQueue, allowing a retry and an in-flight send to race on the same cell and corrupt it. All retries now route through enqueueMessageWrite, which serialises access and uses appendMessageV3's built-in dedup — also eliminating the double-counted totalMessages bug.

changes.title: Changelog index update rate-limited to once per day per client
changes.description: ensureChangelogInRepo rewrote changelogs/index.json on every page load from every concurrent tab. The write is now guarded by a 24-hour localStorage stamp so only the first load per client per day attempts the write, reducing unnecessary API calls.
